weekly_review.narrative:
# Week of March 23-28: Model Review

## WEEK SUMMARY

Rough week. We went 19-25-1 with a -17.2% ROI, and the damage was concentrated in our medium-confidence plays which got absolutely torched at 4-10-1 (-42.4%). The high-confidence bets stayed profitable at 2-1 (+27.3%), but we only had three of them. When your medium tier collapses like this, it drags everything down.

## WHAT WORKED

The high-confidence plays did their job. Small sample at just three bets, but 2-1 with strong ROI suggests the model's conviction threshold is calibrated correctly — when it sees a clear edge, it's usually right.

Our biggest win (Detroit at New Orleans over 226.5) shows the model handling pace-up spots well. New Orleans was playing at a blistering 103.4 possessions per game with offensive efficiency at 115.2, Detroit came in on a back-to-back at 101.5 pace themselves with 114.6 offensive efficiency. Both defenses were leaky (New Orleans at 111.7 defensive efficiency, Detroit at 107.2). The model projected 233.7, final was 237.0. Clean read on a game that had all the ingredients for scoring.

## WHAT DIDN'T

Golden State at Dallas under 230.5 was a disaster — our worst miss of the season. We projected 207.0, the game hit 268.0, a 61-point gap. It went to overtime (regulation total was 252.0, so we were already way off before the extra period). 

Here's the thing: the feature data actually supported a lower total. Dallas was playing at just 93.5 pace, Golden State at 102.6. Both defenses were struggling (Dallas at 118.2 defensive efficiency, Golden State at 117.0), but both offenses were merely solid (Dallas 105.4, Golden State 111.1). Nothing in the inputs screamed 268 points. This feels like the model got caught by game script or variance it couldn't see — maybe a shootout that fed on itself, maybe foul trouble changing rotations. Either way, the features don't explain the miss.

The medium-confidence tier bleeding out at 4-10-1 is concerning. That's where most of our volume lives, and when it runs this cold, we can't overcome it.

## MODEL HEALTH

Zero closing line value across the board (0% positive CLV) is a red flag. We're not finding any market inefficiency, which means we're either betting into sharp numbers or our timing is off. The -17.2% ROI with no CLV suggests we might be fighting market consensus without an actual edge.

No obvious directional bias in the projections, but that 61-point miss on the under is an outlier worth investigating. If the model is systematically underestimating variance in certain pace/efficiency combinations, we need to know.

## HEADING INTO NEXT WEEK

Watch the medium-confidence bucket closely. At 4-10-1, it's either running through bad variance or there's something broken in how we're sizing that tier. If we see another week of medium bets underperforming while high-confidence stays solid, we might need to tighten the medium threshold or reduce exposure there.

Also keeping an eye on that CLV. Zero market beat rate over a full week isn't sustainable for profitable betting.
